mechanism_narrative: Falling unemployment rate strengthens household income and consumer spending, boosting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism is always active across economic regimes.
failure_condition: If the rank correlation between monthly changes in UNRATE and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p>0.10) over any 2-year rolling window, or if the directional accuracy of falling UNRATE predicting positive returns falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, it signals an aggressively tight monetary stance that raises discount rates and suppresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of months when FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or if the average return in such periods is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in both retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signal a broad-based demand contraction that rapidly translates into weaker earnings and negative S&P 500 returns within 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: If joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST does not yield negative 20-day forward returns in at least 60% of occurrences over any 5-year window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During and immediately after NBER-dated recessions, a sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) signals that the supply side is healing, which boosts investor confidence and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rising from a recession trough
failure_condition: If during NBER recession months and the following 6 months, rising INDPRO predicts positive 20-day SPX returns with less than 70% accuracy over any two-recession sample, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index level exceeds 310 (indicating high inflation), market participants anticipate aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when CPIAUCSL > 310, or if the average return is not negative with statistical significance (one-sided t-test p<0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP (GDPC1) indicates a healthy demand environment that raises corporate sales and earnings, consistently supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter regardless of short-term fluctuations.
failure_condition: If the quarterly change in GDPC1 shows a rank correlation below 0.10 with subsequent 20-day SPX returns over any 10-year rolling window, or if the directional accuracy of positive GDP growth predicting positive returns falls below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and retail sales (RSAFS) reinforce each other as a signal of robust consumer-driven growth, leading to immediate positive S&P 500 returns within one week as markets price in stronger earnings.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS and RSAFS both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: If joint increases in PAYEMS and RSAFS do not yield positive 5-day forward returns in at least 65% of occurrences over any 3-year window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market defined by unemployment below 4%, further declines in UNRATE raise the probability of accelerated wage inflation and Fed tightening, which outweighs any positive demand effect and leads to negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If in periods when UNRATE < 4%, falling UNRATE does not predict negative 20-day SPX returns with accuracy above 55% over any 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.
